description: Smart merge layer for upstream use-case updates. When use-case-sync finds an installed use-case with a new repo version, this skill enumerates every file the use-case installs (user.md snippets, skill files, scripts, configs, brain templates) and does a three-way merge per file (local vs ancestor vs upstream). Clean changes auto-apply silently. Real conflicts with local customization trigger plan mode and a consolidated admin notification. Org-agnostic — reads notification config from /agent/brain/permissions/config.json, degrades to file-only mode when Slack is not connected.
post-install: INSTALLER PROTOCOL — follow each step in order. Do not declare the install done until every step is resolved or explicitly waived by the admin.

1. ADMIN GUARD — verify the installing user is a confirmed admin per /agent/brain/permissions/admins.md. If not, route through the org-change request flow in /agent/brain/permissions/user_mode.md and stop.

2. Verify the use-case-sync routine is in place — update-and-merge runs as part of the daily use-case-sync routine, it doesn't have its own reminder. Run `reminder list` and confirm there is an active reminder named 'use-case-sync' (typically cron 0 9 * * *). If missing or inactive, the use-case-sync install should have created it; surface this to the admin and offer to add it with `reminder add --name use-case-sync --cron '0 9 * * *' --timezone <admin-tz> --content 'Run the daily use-case-sync routine. See /agent/.agents/skills/use-case-sync/SKILL.md.'` before continuing. If there are duplicate use-case-sync reminders, surface that too — admin should delete the extras.

3. Patch use-case-sync Step 6 — read /agent/.agents/skills/use-case-sync/SKILL.md. Find the section that currently sends the legacy 'Want me to upgrade it?' Slack ping for update_available items. Replace it with a handoff to update-and-merge in scan mode (see SKILL.md → 'Modifications to existing use-case-sync'). Show the admin the diff and confirm before applying. Skip if already patched (look for 'update-and-merge' in Step 6).

4. Run the skill's setup mode to configure conflict notifications. Walk the admin through: (a) is Slack connected? If yes, ask them to pick a channel from `slack memberships list` and write it to /agent/brain/permissions/config.json under admin_slack_channel. (b) If Slack is not connected, proactively suggest connecting so they actually see conflicts in real time. (c) If they decline Slack entirely, confirm file-only mode in plain language.

5. Verify OPENAI_API_KEY is reachable for the user.md snippet semantic conflict-check step. If not, request via secret-input widget scoped to api.openai.com. In most Motion workspaces this is pre-provisioned.
6. Add an update-and-merge row to /agent/INDEX.md if maintained in this sandbox. The behavior-snippet has been auto-injected so the conversation-level agent already knows about the routine.

7. Confirm to the admin in plain language: 'You're set up. From now on: each morning at 9am, I'll check Motion-Creative/runneth-apps for updates to your installed use-cases. If something is safe to take, I'll quietly bring it in. If there's an actual conflict with changes your team has made, you'll get a writeup and three options to choose from — just reply in plain language.'

post-install.name: Introduce the new capability
post-install.description: Read post-install-intro.md from this use case folder. After all prior install and post-install steps complete, surface the file's sections to the user in chat as the closing message of the install turn: '## What just opened up' as prose, '## Try this now' as a numbered prompt list, '## Compounds with' only when present and at least one named use case is installed in the sandbox. Resolve any {{TOKEN}} placeholders using the same customize map applied during install.

changelog.notes: Added post-install intro. After install completes, Runneth surfaces a short 'what just opened up + try this now' message sourced from post-install-intro.md.

Initial release. Scan mode (called by use-case-sync per installed use-case with update_available): enumerates every file the use-case installs, runs a three-way merge per file (local vs ancestor vs upstream), and either auto-applies clean changes or writes a consolidated plan file when conflicts exist. user.md snippet blocks use LLM semantic conflict check against adjacent user-written rules. Regular files use three-way textual diff against ancestor. Ancestor resolved from installed.json's installed_commit_sha (preferred), commit history walk against installed version (fallback), or conservative-flag mode (when ancestor is unknown). Resolve mode interprets natural-language admin replies and applies per-file choices. Setup mode handles Slack channel config or file-only fallback. Org-agnostic: no hardcoded channel IDs, workspace IDs, or admin names.
